title: Use cause-specific competing-risks survival instead of a single generic stop hazard
summary: A single hazard conflates different pit causes: normal degradation, dry-race rule debt, and opportunistic caution/weather stops.
rationale: A single hazard conflates different pit causes: normal degradation, dry-race rule debt, and opportunistic caution/weather stops. Competing-risks methods are explicitly designed for mutually exclusive event causes and avoid bias from treating other causes as ordinary censoring.
implementation_hint: Assign weak cause labels at actual pit laps using causal heuristics: `degradation_stop`, `rule_debt_stop`, `regime_opportunistic_stop`. Train cause-specific discrete-time hazards or cumulative incidence experts, then sum the next-lap cause probabilities to recover `PitNextLap`.
expected_effect: Should separate orderly planned stops from exogenous spikes, reducing overconfident mistakes in rare regimes and late-race legality cases.
risk: Cause labels will be noisy. If the heuristic taxonomy is wrong, performance can drop; validate each cause model separately and keep the final aggregation simple.
